narrative: Our open-source research community has seen significant progress across multiple projects, with a focus on enhancing stability, functionality, and user experience. The 'scienceverse/metacheck' and 'JamieCummins/regcheck' repositories have made notable strides in bug fixes, feature additions, and code improvements, while 'giladfeldman/docpluck' has refined its document processing capabilities. Meanwhile, 'Davidvandijcke/coarse' has refined its web interface and models, and 'giladfeldman/escicheck' has maintained consistency with the 'escicheckapp' repository through sync commits. Additionally, 'shaheedazaad/auto-zcurve' has polished its documentation, highlighting the community's commitment to academic rigor and credibility. These connections and advancements demonstrate the community's collaborative spirit and dedication to advancing open-source research tools.

repos.summary: Recent development activity for the 'JamieCummins/regcheck' GitHub repository has focused on enhancing the user interface, improving report functionality, and adding API support. The commits indicate a significant overhaul of the report page, including redesigns, aesthetic updates, and improvements to report navigation, editing, and security. Additionally, the repository has seen updates to the API configuration, the addition of OAuth implementation, and various bug fixes, suggesting a direction towards a more robust, user-friendly, and secure application. Overall, the development activity suggests a major refinement of the repository's core features and user experience.

recent_commits.message: improve security and sharing of reports
recent_commits.author: Jamie Cummins

repos.summary: Recent development activity for the 'scienceverse/metacheck' GitHub repository has focused on bug fixes, code improvements, and feature additions. Specifically, commits have addressed issues with validation, loops, and code checks, while also adding new functionality such as sanitizing steps and support for Zenodo repositories. The overall direction of development appears to be aimed at enhancing the stability and functionality of the metacheck tool, with multiple contributors working to resolve bugs and improve its performance. Additionally, there have been efforts to restructure internal functions and update code for specific use cases, such as OSF preprint lists and report validation.

repos.summary: Recent development activity in the 'giladfeldman/docpluck' GitHub repository has focused on enhancing the rendering, normalization, and table handling capabilities of the project. The overall direction of development appears to be aimed at improving the accuracy and consistency of document processing, with a series of incremental updates and fixes being implemented to achieve this goal.

repos.summary: Recent development activity for the GitHub repository 'Davidvandijcke/coarse' has focused on refining the web interface, models, and pipeline functionality. Specifically, updates have included adding new models such as Claude Fable 5, recalibrating cost estimates for reasoning models, and improving the web interface with features like per-comment Discuss chat streaming and defaulting to latest host models.  The same comparison can run entirely on your own machine. Metacheck bundles

+[Matilda Fogato](https://github.com/Matilda03/regcheck-fork)) together with

files.patch:
@@ -1,7 +1,7 @@
 Package: effectcheck
 Type: Package
 Title: Statistical Consistency Checker for Published Research Results
-Version: 0.6.1
+Version: 0.6.2
 Authors@R: c(person(given = "Gilad", family = "Feldman", role = c("aut", "cre"),
     email = "giladfel@gmail.com", comment = c(ORCID = "0000-0003-2812-6599")))
 Description: A conservative, assumption-aware statistical consistency checker

@@ -1,3 +1,30 @@
+# effectcheck 0.6.2
+
+Exact binomial test reported with Cohen's h. New `test_type = "binomial"`
+matched via `pat_binom_h`, anchored on a "binomial p [op] <pval>" clause
+followed (within ~80 non-period chars) by "Cohen('s)? h = <h>". When a
+"<n> out of <N>" clause is present in the same verbatim, N is recovered
+(`N_source = "binom_n_out_of_N"`) and check.R re-computes the two-sided
+binomial p via `stats::binom.test()` assuming p_null = 0.5 (the most
+common null in binomia

@@ -2922,6 +2922,51 @@ compute_and_compare_one <- function(row,
       paste("Cochran Q reported as a meta-analytic heterogeneity test -",
             "no standard effect size; consistency check is on the reported",
             "p-value vs chi-square(Q, df). I-squared not independently verified."))
+  } else if (tt == "binomial") {
+    # ------ EXACT BINOMIAL TEST WITH COHEN'S H ------
+    # v0.6.2: When the verbatim is "<n> out of <N> ... binomial p [op] X,
+    # Cohen's h = Y", the parser

@@ -726,6 +726,26 @@ parse_text <- function(text, context_window_size = 2) {
   #   homogeneity null with the reported df). The "_<letter>" subscript is
   #   optional; the df may be in [brackets] or (parens) with optional spaces.
   pat_cochran_q <- "\\bQ(?:_[A-Za-z])?\\s*[\\[(]\\s*(\\d+(?:\\.\\d+)?)\\s*[\\])]\\s*=\\s*([-+]?\\d*\\.?\\d+)"
+  # v0.6.2: exact binomial test with Cohen's h effect size.
+  # Form: "(exact )?binomial p [op] <pval>[, ]Cohen('s)? h = <h>[, 95% CI [<lo>, <hi>]]"
+  # T

@@ -7,7 +7,7 @@
 check_text(
   text,
   stats = c("t", "F", "r", "chisq", "z", "U", "W", "H", "regression", "spearman",
-    "kendall", "kendall_w", "dscf", "cochran_q", "RR", "rdpct", "md_hl"),
+    "kendall", "kendall_w", "dscf", "cochran_q", "RR", "rdpct", "md_hl", "binomial"),
   ci_level = 0.95,
   alpha = 0.05,
   one_tailed = FALSE,

@@ -0,0 +1,68 @@
+# v0.6.2: exact binomial test reported with Cohen's h
+#
+# Form: "<n> out of <N> ... binomial p [op] X, Cohen's h = Y, [95% CI [...]]"
+# Common in decoy-effect / binary-choice replication papers (CRSP, JESP).
+#
+# Before v0.6.2 these returned 0 rows. v0.6.2 adds pat_binom_h with two
+# anchors (binomial p AND Cohen's h within ~80 non-period chars) plus a
+# NOTE-only check.R branch that recomputes the binomial p via binom.test()
+# when n / N are recoverable from a "<n> out
